articles.title: German Inflation Eases as Oil Retreat Tames Prices in Europe
articles.summary: German inflation cooled more than expected in June, part of a slowdown across most of the region brought on by the drop in global oil prices.

articles.title: US Stocks Set to Finish Best Quarter in Six Years: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: US index futures steadied on the last day of a quarter that looks set to be the S&P 500’s best in six years. The dollar climbed as the yen slid to its weakest level since 1986.

articles.title: SpaceX IPO Left Mirae With No Shares on Misunderstanding
articles.summary: It was supposed to be the deal that vaulted Mirae Asset Securities Co. into the global big leagues.

articles.title: AI Leverage Is More Worrying Than Valuations, IMF’s Adrian Says
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence debt issuance may point to more of a financial stability concern than stock valuations, according to a senior International Monetary Fund official.

articles.title: Copper Traders Gear Up for Another Round of Tariff Turmoil
articles.summary: The US Commerce Department is due to deliver a key report to the White House to inform Donald Trump’s next move.

articles.title: BOE’s Breeden Warns AI Agents Risk Causing Market Meltdowns
articles.summary: Autonomous artificial intelligence agents risk causing “market meltdown” and may need tighter regulation, Bank of England Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden warned.

articles.title: Gold Edges Above $4,000 as Traders Eye Iran Talks, Fed Outlook
articles.summary: Gold edged above $4,000 an ounce, as traders awaited fresh news from peace talks between the US and Iran and clues on the outlook for US monetary policy.

articles.title: Former Governors Team Up to Address AI Job Losses
articles.summary: Former Indiana Governor Eric Holcomb (R) says he is co-chairing an organization called RAISE US that is aimed at easing the American workforce’s transition to an artificial intelligence-based economy.

articles.title: Argentina’s Beef Export Boom to US Leaves Locals Paying More for Steak
articles.summary: Ranchers on the Pampas answer Trump’s call and turn away from China, but the trend doesn’t bode well for Milei as he heads into an election.

articles.title: Europe’s Top Steelmakers Warn Against Weakening Carbon Market
articles.summary: The European Union’s biggest steelmakers, including Salzgitter AG and SSAB AB, urged policy makers to avoid weakening the bloc’s flagship emissions market and to support strengthening its carbon borde
